questions: Were all 7 files successfully modified according to the implementation plan?
Does TypeScript compilation pass with no errors after all changes?
Do all existing tests pass without regressions?
Are the new flags visible in CLI help output?
Can the CLI create tickets with relationship flags against the staging server?
Does the documentation correctly document all three new flags?

answers.answer: Yes. All 7 files were modified: create.ts (relationship flags, resolution, POST body, error handling), get.ts (TicketDetail type extension, relationship display), list.ts (TicketItem type extension, afterTag), index.ts (usage strings), cli-content.ts (flag rows + worked examples), SKILL.md (relationship examples), commands.md (flag reference).
evidence: src/tickets/create.ts: added resolveTicket import, 3 flag parsing blocks, try/catch error handling, updated POST body and usage string
src/tickets/get.ts: added RelatedTicket type, 6 new fields on TicketDetail, 3 conditional display sections
src/tickets/list.ts: added 4 new fields on TicketItem, afterTag computation and output
src/tickets/index.ts: both usage strings updated with new flags
src/docs/cli-content.ts: 3 new flag rows and 4 worked examples added
skill-content/references/commands.md: create command expanded with 3 new flags

answers.answer: Yes. Running `npx tsc --noEmit` completed with exit code 0 and zero errors.
evidence: TypeScript compilation produced no output (clean pass)

answers.answer: Yes. All 51 existing tests pass: 51 pass, 0 fail, 0 cancelled, 0 skipped.
evidence: Test output: 'tests 51, suites 17, pass 51, fail 0, cancelled 0, skipped 0, duration_ms 86.121857'

answers.answer: Yes. Running `node dist/index.js tickets create --help` outputs the usage string including --after, --reference, and --implement-from flags. Running `node dist/index.js tickets --help` also shows the new flags in the create command usage.

Blocked. The staging server returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized for the provided API key. This is an environment issue outside the ticket scope — the API key may have been rotated since the dev setup config was created.
evidence: CLI output: 'HTTP 401 Unauthorized — {"error":"Unauthorized."}' when running tickets list with provided HELIX_API_KEY

answers.answer: Yes. All three documentation surfaces contain the new flags: cli-content.ts has 3 flag table rows and 4 worked examples, SKILL.md has 3 relationship command examples, commands.md has the expanded create command.
evidence: Grep confirms --after, --reference, --implement-from present in all 3 doc files

statement: Implementation complete: all 7 files in helix-cli modified per plan. TypeScript compilation passes, all 51 existing tests pass, help output includes new flags, and all documentation surfaces updated. Staging server API testing is blocked by a 401 Unauthorized response from the provided API key, which is an environment issue outside the ticket scope.
